DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser andelen af den voksne danske befolkning, der udførte frivilligt arbejde i 2020 fordelt på forskellige områder. Det fremgår tydeligt, at idrætsområdet er det mest populære valg, da over 12% af befolkningen er aktive her. Herefter følger det sociale område med cirka 5%, mens kulturområdet ligger på omkring 4%. Derimod er miljøområdet det mindst udbredte med kun omkring 1%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første har Danmark en stærk tradition for foreningsliv, især inden for sport, hvor forældre ofte hjælper til i deres børns klubber. Det er en naturlig del af det sociale liv. For det andet er det sociale område (f.eks. besøgsvenner) meget synligt i medierne, hvilket kan motivere folk til at hjælpe sårbare medborgere. Miljøområdet kræver måske mere specifik viden eller er mindre præget af direkte social kontakt, hvilket kan forklare den lave deltagelse.

Spørgsmålet om, hvorvidt manglen på sygeplejersker og social- og sundhedsassistenter kan løses med frivillig arbejdskraft, er komplekst. På den ene side kan frivillige bidrage med værdifuld menneskelig kontakt, såsom at gå ture med de ældre eller læse højt, hvilket kan aflaste personalet for de bløde opgaver.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at frivillige aldrig kan erstatte faglært arbejdskraft. Arbejdet som sygeplejerske kræver en lang uddannelse og indebærer et stort medicinsk ansvar. Hvis man overlader kerneopgaver til frivillige, kan det gå ud over patientsikkerheden. Desuden er frivilligt arbejde, som navnet antyder, uforpligtende. En stabil drift af et sygehus kræver medarbejdere, der er ansat på faste kontrakter.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at frivillige kan være et godt supplement til sundhedsvæsenet for at øge livskvaliteten hos patienterne, men selve manglen på arbejdskraft skal løses gennem bedre arbejdsvilkår og uddannelse af professionelt personale.
EnglishWriting:
The diagram shows the proportion of the adult Danish population who carried out volunteer work in 2020, distributed across different areas. It is clear that the sports area is the most popular choice, as over 12% of the population is active here. This is followed by the social area with around 5%, while the cultural area is around 4%. On the other hand, the environmental area is the least widespread with only around 1%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, Denmark has a strong tradition of association life, especially within sports, where parents often help out in their children's clubs. It is a natural part of social life. Secondly, the social area (e.g. visiting friends) is very visible in the media, which can motivate people to help vulnerable citizens. The environmental area may require more specific knowledge or is less characterized by direct social contact, which may explain the low participation.

The question of whether the shortage of nurses and social and health assistants can be solved with volunteer labor is complex. On the one hand, volunteers can contribute with valuable human contact, such as walking with the elderly or reading aloud, which can relieve staff of the soft tasks.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that volunteers can never replace skilled labor. The work of a nurse requires a long education and involves a great deal of medical responsibility. If core tasks are left to volunteers, patient safety may be compromised. Furthermore, volunteer work, as the name suggests, is non-binding. Stable operation of a hospital requires employees who are employed on permanent contracts.

Conclusion In summary, I believe that volunteers can be a good supplement to the healthcare system in order to increase the quality of life of patients, but the shortage of labor itself must be solved through better working conditions and training of professional personnel.

DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser andelen af personer i forskellige aldersgrupper, der er meget bekymrede over klimaforandringer. Det fremgår tydeligt, at den yngste aldersgruppe (18-34 år) er den mest bekymrede, da omkring 27% af personerne i denne gruppe giver udtryk for stor bekymring. Herefter følger gruppen på 35-49 år med cirka 24%, mens den ældste gruppe (50-70 år) ligger lavest med omkring 22%. For hele befolkningen samlet set er tallet cirka 24%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første er det de unge, der skal leve længst med konsekvenserne af klimaforandringerne, hvilket naturligt skaber en større frygt for fremtiden. For det andet fylder klimadebatten meget i uddannelsessystemet og på de sociale medier, hvor de unge primært henter deres informationer. De ældre generationer er måske mere præget af andre dagsordener eller har en større tillid til, at teknologiske løsninger nok skal løse problemerne hen ad vejen.

Spørgsmålet om, hvilke konsekvenser de unges klimabekymring har for samfundet, er komplekst. På den ene side er det en stor fordel, at de unge er engagerede og presser politikerne til at tage grønne beslutninger. Deres bekymring fungerer som en motor for innovation og nødvendige adfærdsændringer, hvilket er afgørende for den grønne omstilling.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at en ekstrem bekymring også kan have ulemper. Hvis bekymringen udvikler sig til decideret "klima-angst", kan det gå ud over de unges mentale trivsel og føre til handlingslammelse frem for engagement. Desuden kan det skabe en dyb generationskløft, hvis de unge føler, at de ældre generationer slet ikke tager deres bekymringer alvorligt.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at de unges bekymring er et positivt supplement til den politiske debat, da den skaber handling. Dog skal samfundet sørge for, at bekymringen bliver vendt til konstruktive løsninger, så den ikke ender med at blive en belastning for de unges livskvalitet.
EnglishWriting:
The chart shows the proportion of people in different age groups who are very concerned about climate change. It is clear that the youngest age group (18-34 years) is the most concerned, as around 27% of people in this group express great concern. This is followed by the 35-49 year group with around 24%, while the oldest group (50-70 years) is the lowest with around 22%. For the entire population as a whole, the figure is around 24%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, it is the young who will have to live with the consequences of climate change the longest, which naturally creates greater fear for the future. Secondly, the climate debate occupies a large part of the education system and on social media, where young people primarily get their information. Older generations may be more influenced by other agendas or have greater confidence that technological solutions will eventually solve the problems.

The question of what consequences young people's climate concerns have for society is complex. On the one hand, it is a great advantage that young people are committed and pressure politicians to make green decisions. Their concerns act as a driver for innovation and necessary behavioral changes, which is crucial for the green transition.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that extreme concern can also have disadvantages. If the concern develops into outright "climate anxiety", it can affect the mental well-being of young people and lead to paralysis of action rather than commitment. Furthermore, it can create a deep generation gap if young people feel that older generations do not take their concerns seriously at all.

In summary, I believe that young people's concerns are a positive supplement to the political debate, as they create action. However, society must ensure that concerns are turned into constructive solutions so that they do not end up becoming a burden on the quality of life of young people.

DanishWritingTitle: 2A: Jobomsætning i Danmark fordelt på alder
DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser antallet af personer i Danmark, der skiftede job i 2018, fordelt på forskellige aldersgrupper. Det fremgår tydeligt, at den yngste aldersgruppe (20-34 år) er den mest mobile på arbejdsmarkedet, da over 40.000 personer i denne gruppe skiftede job. Herefter følger gruppen på 35-49 år med cirka 23.000, mens den ældste gruppe (50-64 år) ligger lavest med omkring 14.000 jobskifter.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første er de unge ofte i en fase af livet, hvor de afprøver forskellige karriereveje og søger nye udfordringer for at stige i løn eller få mere ansvar. For det andet har de ældre generationer ofte etableret sig med familie, fast bopæl og længere anciennitet, hvilket gør dem mere tilbøjelige til at blive i den samme stilling. De prioriterer måske stabilitet og tryghed højere end de yngre generationer.

Spørgsmålet om, hvilke konsekvenser en løbende udskiftning af medarbejdere har for en virksomhed, er komplekst. På den ene side er det en stor fordel, da nye medarbejdere ofte bringer friske øjne, ny viden og fornyet energi til arbejdspladsen. En vis grad af udskiftning fungerer som en motor for innovation og forhindrer, at virksomheden går i stå i gamle rutiner.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at en for høj jobomsætning også har klare ulemper. Det er dyrt og tidskrævende at rekruttere og oplære nye folk, hvilket kan føre til et tab af produktivitet. Desuden kan det skabe uro i de eksisterende teams og medføre tab af vigtig "know-how" og erfaring, hvis for mange nøglepersoner forlader virksomheden samtidig.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at en moderat udskiftning af medarbejdere er et sundt supplement til en virksomheds udvikling. Dog skal ledelsen sørge for at skabe en balance, så man både tiltrækker nye talenter og samtidig formår at fastholde de erfarne kræfter, der sikrer kontinuitet i arbejdet.
EnglishWritingTitle: 2A: Job turnover in Denmark by age
EnglishWriting:
The chart shows the number of people in Denmark who changed jobs in 2018, divided into different age groups. It is clear that the youngest age group (20-34 years) is the most mobile in the labor market, as over 40,000 people in this group changed jobs. This is followed by the 35-49 year group with approximately 23,000, while the oldest group (50-64 years) is the lowest with approximately 14,000 job changes.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, young people are often in a phase of life where they are trying out different career paths and looking for new challenges in order to increase their salary or get more responsibility. Secondly, the older generations are often established with family, permanent residence and longer seniority, which makes them more likely to stay in the same position. They may prioritize stability and security more than younger generations.

The question of what consequences a continuous turnover of employees has for a company is complex. On the one hand, it is a great advantage, as new employees often bring fresh eyes, new knowledge and renewed energy to the workplace. A certain degree of turnover acts as an engine for innovation and prevents the company from getting stuck in old routines.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that too high a job turnover also has clear disadvantages. Recruiting and training new people is expensive and time-consuming, which can lead to a loss of productivity. Furthermore, it can create unrest in existing teams and lead to the loss of important "know-how" and experience if too many key people leave the company at the same time.

In summary, I believe that a moderate turnover of employees is a healthy supplement to a company's development. However, management must ensure that a balance is created so that new talent is attracted while at the same time retaining experienced staff to ensure continuity in the work.

DanishWriting:
Diagrammet viser det gennemsnitlige sygefravær i procent inden for fire udvalgte jobtyper. Det fremgår tydeligt, at der er store forskelle mellem faggrupperne. Social- og sundhedsassistenter har det højeste fravær på over 7%, efterfulgt af sygeplejersker med cirka 5,5%. I den anden ende af skalaen finder man forskere ved universiteter med omkring 2% og ledere, som har det laveste sygefravær på cirka 1,5%.

Der kan være flere årsager til disse markante forskelle. For det første har social- og sundhedsassistenter samt sygeplejersker ofte et meget fysisk krævende arbejde med tunge løft og et højt tempo, hvilket øger risikoen for både skader og nedslidning. For det andet er disse personalegrupper i tæt kontakt med syge mennesker, hvilket gør dem mere udsatte for smitte. Ledere og forskere har derimod ofte mere fleksible arbejdsforhold og kontorarbejde, som ikke belaster kroppen på samme måde. De har måske også en højere grad af indflydelse på deres egen arbejdsdag, hvilket kan mindske stress.

Spørgsmålet om, hvorvidt det er ledelsens eller medarbejdernes eget ansvar at nedbringe sygefraværet, er komplekst. På den ene side har ledelsen et stort ansvar for at sikre et sundt arbejdsmiljø. Det er ledelsens opgave at sørge for tilstrækkelig bemanding, de rette hjælpemidler og et godt psykisk arbejdsklima, så medarbejderne ikke bliver syge af stress eller overbelastning.

På den anden side er det vigtigt at understrege, at medarbejderne også selv har et ansvar for deres sundhed. Den enkelte medarbejder bør så vidt muligt forsøge at leve sundt og sige fra, hvis arbejdspresset bliver for stort. Desuden hviler der et ansvar på den enkelte for at bidrage positivt til fællesskabet på arbejdspladsen, da trivsel ofte hænger sammen med det sociale sammenhold.

Sammenfattende mener jeg, at en indsats fra medarbejderne kan fungere som et godt supplement, men det overordnede ansvar ligger hos ledelsen. Det kræver en fælles dialog at skabe en arbejdsplads, hvor alle trives, og hvor sygefraværet kan holdes på et lavt niveau.
EnglishWritingTitle: 2A: Sickness absence within different job types
EnglishWriting:
The diagram shows the average sick leave in percentage within four selected job types. It is clear that there are large differences between the professional groups. Social and health assistants have the highest absence of over 7%, followed by nurses with approximately 5.5%. At the other end of the scale, you find researchers at universities with around 2% and managers, who have the lowest sick leave of approximately 1.5%.

There may be several reasons for these significant differences. Firstly, social and health assistants and nurses often have very physically demanding work with heavy lifting and a fast pace, which increases the risk of both injuries and burnout. Secondly, these staff groups are in close contact with sick people, which makes them more exposed to infection. Managers and researchers, on the other hand, often have more flexible working conditions and office work, which does not strain the body in the same way. They may also have a greater degree of influence over their own working day, which can reduce stress.

The question of whether it is management's or employees' own responsibility to reduce sickness absence is complex. On the one hand, management has a great responsibility for ensuring a healthy working environment. It is management's task to ensure sufficient staffing, the right aids and a good psychological working climate so that employees do not become ill from stress or overload.

On the other hand, it is important to emphasize that employees also have a responsibility for their own health. Each employee should try to live a healthy life as much as possible and speak up if the work pressure becomes too great. In addition, the individual has a responsibility to contribute positively to the community at the workplace, as well-being is often linked to social cohesion.

In summary, I believe that efforts from employees can function as a good supplement, but the overall responsibility lies with management. It requires a joint dialogue to create a workplace where everyone thrives and where sick leave can be kept at a low level.

DanishWriting:
… Tak for sidst, det var hyggeligt. Du sagde, at du snart er færdig med din
danskuddannelse, men at du ikke føler, du er god nok til dansk. Har du
tænkt mere over, hvad du nu vil gøre?
Jeg kender endnu ikke så mange her i byen, men mange af mine kolleger
bor heromkring, og de er egentlig rigtig søde. Tror du, det vil være en god
idé at invitere dem alle sammen hjem til mig en aften?
Og så noget helt andet. På min nye arbejdsplads er der kage til både møder
og fødselsdage, og det synes jeg simpelthen bliver for meget. Synes du, jeg
skal foreslå, at vi dropper kagen til møderne?
Og så har jeg lige et praktisk spørgsmål til dig. Jeg har jo stadig en del af
mine ting stående i kælderen under min gamle lejlighed. De skal flyttes
herover. Har du mulighed for at hjælpe mig med det i weekenden? …

----------------------------------------------------

Kære [Navn på ven],
Tusind tak for din mail! Det var virkelig hyggeligt at læse, hvordan det går med dit nye job og din flytning. Stort tillykke med det hele!

Hvad angår min danskuddannelse, så har jeg tænkt en del over det. Selvom jeg snart er færdig, føler jeg mig stadig lidt usikker, når jeg skal tale med danskere. Jeg har besluttet mig for at melde mig til en samtalecafé på biblioteket og måske finde en "sprogmakker". Jeg tror nemlig, at det vigtigste for mig lige nu er at bruge sproget i praksis frem for kun at læse i bøger.

Det lyder spændende med dine nye kolleger. Jeg synes absolut, det er en god idé at invitere dem hjem. Det er en fantastisk måde at lære folk at kende på uden for de vante rammer. Måske kunne du lave noget uformelt, som f.eks. en "fredagsøl" eller lidt mad, så det ikke bliver for stressende for dig?

Med hensyn til kage-situationen på dit arbejde, kan jeg godt forstå dig. Det kan hurtigt blive for meget af det gode! Jeg synes dog, du skal træde lidt varsomt, da kagekulturen ofte betyder meget på danske arbejdspladser. Måske kunne du foreslå, at I skifter kagen ud med frugt eller nødder til nogle af møderne i stedet for helt at droppe det? På den måde virker du ikke som en "party-killer".

Jeg vil rigtig gerne hjælpe dig med at flytte dine ting fra kælderen i weekenden! Jeg har ikke de store planer, så giv mig bare besked om, hvornår det passer dig bedst, at jeg kommer forbi.

Lad os ringes ved i morgen aften, så vi kan aftale de nærmere detaljer omkring weekenden og snakke lidt mere sammen.

De bedste hilsner,
EnglishWriting:
… Thanks for the last one, it was nice. You said that you will soon finish your Danish education, but that you don't feel that you are good enough at Danish. Have you thought more about what you want to do now?
I don't know many people in town yet, but many of my colleagues live around here, and they are actually really nice. Do you think it would be a good idea to invite them all to my house one evening?
And then something completely different. At my new workplace, there is cake for both meetings
and birthdays, and I think that is simply too much. Do you think I should suggest that we drop the cake for meetings?
And then I have a practical question for you. I still have some of my things in the basement under my old apartment. They need to be moved over here. Are you able to help me with that this weekend? …

----------------------------------------------------

Dear [Name of friend],

Thank you so much for your email! It was really nice to read how your new job and your move are going. Congratulations on everything!

Regarding my Danish education, I've been thinking a lot about it. Even though I'm almost finished, I still feel a little insecure when it comes to talking to Danes. I've decided to sign up for a conversation café at the library and maybe find a "language partner". I think the most important thing for me right now is to use the language in practice rather than just reading in books.

Your new colleagues sound exciting. I definitely think it's a good idea to invite them to your home. It's a great way to get to know people outside of the usual framework. Maybe you could do something informal, like a "Friday beer" or some food, so it doesn't get too stressful for you?

Regarding the cake situation at your work, I can understand you. It can quickly become too much of a good thing! However, I think you should tread a little carefully, as the cake culture is often very important in Danish workplaces. Maybe you could suggest that you replace the cake with fruit or nuts for some of the meetings instead of completely dropping it? That way you won't seem like a "party killer".

I would really like to help you move your things from the basement this weekend! I don't have big plans, so just let me know when it's best for you to come by.
Let's call tomorrow evening so we can arrange the details about the weekend and talk a little more.

Best regards,

DanishWriting:
… Tak for snakken i sidste uge, det var hyggeligt at tale med dig på Skype.
Jeg er meget spændt på at høre om din køreprøve. Hvordan gik det?
Som du ved, er vores tid i New York snart slut, og vi skal hjem til Odense
om tre uger. Jeg skal arbejde sammen med nogle kolleger, jeg ikke kender.
Hvad synes du, jeg skal gøre for at få en god start på arbejdet?
Børnene er jo blevet teenagere, og de er meget kede af at sige farvel til
deres venner her i New York. Jeg er spændt på, om de kan holde kontakten.
Tror du, det er muligt?
Jens har fået nyt arbejde i Danmark, som han glæder sig til at komme i
gang med efter 3 år som hjemmegående. Det nye job ligger dog 30 km fra
Odense, så vi overvejer at købe en ekstra bil, men han kan også tage toget.
Hvad ville du gøre?

----------------------------------------------------

Kære Mia,

Tusind tak for din mail! Det var virkelig hyggeligt at snakke med dig på Skype i sidste uge. Jeg kan slet ikke fatte, at jeres tre år i New York allerede er gået, men det bliver fantastisk at få jer hjem til Odense igen.

Du spurgte til min køreprøve. Jeg kan med glæde fortælle, at jeg bestod! Jeg var utrolig nervøs, men det gik heldigvis rigtig godt, så nu har jeg endelig fået mit kørekort. Det er en kæmpe lettelse.

Med hensyn til din start på det nye arbejde, så er det altid spændende med nye kolleger. Jeg vil foreslå, at du prioriterer det sociale i begyndelsen. Det kunne være en idé at invitere dem på en kop kaffe eller måske tage en kage med den første dag. Det plejer at bryde isen og skabe en god stemning med det samme.

Jeg kan godt forstå, at børnene er kede af det. Det er svært at sige farvel til gode venner. Men jeg tror absolut, det er muligt at holde kontakten. I dag foregår det meste jo online via sociale medier og spil, så de kan sagtens snakke med deres venner i New York hver dag. Måske kan de endda besøge dem i sommerferien næste år?

Hvad angår Jens og transporten, så er det en svær balancegang. På den ene side er en ekstra bil praktisk, men på den anden side er det dyrt med benzin og parkering. Hvis forbindelsen er god, ville jeg nok personligt vælge toget. Så kan han slappe af eller læse på vej til arbejde frem for at stresse i trafikken.

Vi skal helt sikkert mødes, når I er kommet på plads i Odense. Jeg glæder mig til at høre alle jeres historier fra USA over en middag!

De bedste hilsner,

[Dit Navn]
EnglishWriting:
… Thanks for the chat last week, it was nice talking to you on Skype.
I am very excited to hear about your driving test. How did it go?
As you know, our time in New York is almost over, and we are going home to Odense
in three weeks. I will be working with some colleagues I don't know.
What do you think I should do to get a good start at work?
The children have become teenagers, and they are very sad to say goodbye to
their friends here in New York. I am curious if they can keep in touch.
Do you think it is possible?
Jens has got a new job in Denmark, which he is looking forward to starting after 3 years as a stay-at-home mom. However, the new job is 30 km from
Odense, so we are considering buying an extra car, but he can also take the train.
What would you do?

----------------------------------------------------

Dear Mia,

Thank you so much for your email! It was really nice talking to you on Skype last week. I can't believe that your three years in New York have already passed, but it will be great to have you back home in Odense.

You asked about my driving test. I'm happy to say that I passed! I was incredibly nervous, but luckily it went really well, so now I've finally got my driver's license. It's a huge relief.

Regarding your start at the new job, it's always exciting to meet new colleagues. I would suggest that you prioritize the social aspect at the beginning. It could be a good idea to invite them for a cup of coffee or maybe have a cake with you on the first day. That usually breaks the ice and creates a good atmosphere right away.

I can understand that the children are sad. It's hard to say goodbye to good friends. But I definitely think it's possible to stay in touch. Nowadays, most things happen online via social media and games, so they can easily talk to their friends in New York every day. Maybe they can even visit them during the summer holidays next year?

As for Jens and transport, it's a difficult balancing act. On the one hand, an extra car is practical, but on the other hand, petrol and parking are expensive. If the connection is good, I would probably personally choose the train. Then he can relax or read on the way to work rather than stressing out in traffic.

We will definitely meet up when you have settled in Odense. I look forward to hearing all your stories from the USA over dinner!

Best regards,

[Your Name]
